children.name: Lycosa tarantula
children.description: The original tarantula -- name derives from Taranto in southern Italy. Historically blamed for tarantism. The New World theraphosids later borrowed the name.

children.name: Hogna carolinensis
children.description: Largest wolf spider in North America; body up to 35mm. Female carries egg sac on spinnerets; spiderlings ride on her back.

children.name: Pardosa amentata
children.description: Extremely abundant in Sweden; on shores of lakes, bogs, and wet meadows. Females carry egg sacs and guard spiderlings. Males perform leg-waving courtship.

children.name: Pardosa lugubris
children.description: Common woodland wolf spider across Europe including Sweden; active on forest floor in spring and early summer.

children.name: Pirata piraticus
children.description: Semiaquatic; capable of walking on water. Common in Swedish bogs and lakeshores. Named pirate for its ability to sail on water surfaces.
